COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 62845448) FILED BY PLAINTIFF TAYLOR, JARED AN INDIVIDUAL NEW CENTURY FOUNDATION A KENTUCKY NOT-FOR-PROFIT TRUST, ON BEHALF OF THEMSELVES, THOSE SIMILARLY SITUATED, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JARED TAYLOR, NEW CENTURY FOUNDATION'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 425.16. ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS JARED TAYLOR AND NEW CENTURY FOUNDATION'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEE PER CCP 425.16 IS DENIED. THE UNSUCCESSFUL ARGUMENTS MADE BY DEFENDANT TWITTER, INC. WHY THE CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFFS WERE NOT COVERED BY THE PUBLIC INTEREST EXCEPTION TO THE ACTIVITY PROTECTED BY THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE WERE NEITHER TOTALLY AND COMPLETELY WITHOUT MERIT NOR SOLELY INTENDED TO DELAY THIS CASE. TWITTER'S ARGUMENTS FALL WELL WITHIN THE BROAD RANGE OF PERMISSIBLE AND NON-SANCTIONABLE ARGUMENTS THAT A PARTY MAY MAKE. ALTHOUGH THE COURT REJECTED THOSE ARGUMENTS, THE ARGUMENTS WERE BASED ON REASONED ANALYSIS OF THE STATUTORY TEXT, THE PURPOSE OF THE PUBLIC INTEREST EXCEPTION, AND CASE LAW CONSTRUING THAT EXCEPTION. COUNSEL FOR TWITTER IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AS TO FORM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600 EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

Notice Of Motion And Motion For For Attorney'S Fees Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 425.16
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Noah Peters, Esq., Telephone 202-688-3246, appearing for Plaintiff;
Mark Flnagan, Wilmer Hale, Telephone 650-858-6047, appearing for Defendant Twitter;

Ruling - ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JARED TAYLOR, NEW CENTURY FOUNDATION Notice Of Motion And Motion For For Attorney'S Fees Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 425.16

Plaintiffs Jared Taylor and New Century Foundation's motion for attorneys' fee per CCP 425.16 is denied. The unsuccessful arguments made by defendant Twitter, Inc. why the claims alleged by plaintiffs were not covered by the public interest exception to the activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute were neither totally and completely without merit nor solely intended to delay this case. Twitter's arguments fall well within the broad range of permissible and non-sanctionable arguments that a party may make. Although the court rejected those arguments, the arguments were based on reasoned analysis of the statutory text, the purpose of the public interest exception, and case law construing that exception. Counsel for Twitter is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling as to form and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marc Randazza, Esq. 702-757-1001 of Randazza Legal Group PLLC for Plaintiff Jared Taylor
Noah Peters, Esq. 202-499-4222 specially appearing for Plaintiff Jared Taylor.
Adam Candeub, Esq. 517-918-7147 specially appearing for Plaintiff Jared Taylor
Patrick Carome, Esq. 202-663-6610 and Thomas Sprankling, Esq. 650-858-6062 of Wilmerhale for Defendant Twitter.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court announces the tentative rulings. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend on the two causes of action and the Anti-SLAPP motion is Denied on the grounds that the first prong has not been established by Twitter. Both sides Argue. The Court allows out of state attorneys to speak. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT TWITTER, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFFS JARED TAYLOR AND NEW CENTURY FOUNDATION.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Sustained without leave to amend on the two causes of action. Twitter has 30 days to file an answer.

Defendant to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT TWITTER, INC.'s Verified Application for the Pro Hac Vice Admission of Patrick Carome is GRANTED. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The fairly large number of times that Mr. Carome has been admitted pro hac vice in California courts in the last two years suggests that Mr. Carome should consider seeking admission as a member of the California Bar and/or the time may soon come when this courts may deny applications seeking his pro hac vice admission.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.
